scenario-title: Governance capacity bypass — limited governance capacity gamed by value-tier-aware attacker
scenario-purpose: Bridge to Finding 4 (governance capacity scaling): a governance actor with limited review capacity (cap=3) is gamed by an attacker who selects dispute value tiers that fall below the governance review threshold. The attacker wins because low-value disputes are never reviewed.
scenario-notes: Low-value escrow (100 USDC) where the seller raises a fraudulent dispute. The malicious resolver rules in seller's favour (is-release=false, refunding to seller). With limited governance capacity, this low-value dispute would fall below the review threshold and never be reviewed. The buyer has no recourse but sender_cancel. This bridges the Phase AA Monte Carlo result (20% attacker win-rate breaks under limited capacity) to a deterministic trace. In a multi-epoch simulation, the attacker repeats this pattern to achieve >20% win rate.
